steps.title: Use the passivity clue
steps.content: The subscription model benefits retailers when customers keep paying because they do not cancel. The final sentence names this behavior as passivity, so the blank must mean a tendency not to act.

steps.title: Reject active or unrelated traits
steps.content: Choice B suggests deliberate avoidance, not passive inaction. Choice C means quick, firm action, the opposite of delayed cancellation. Choice D refers to subscriber churn, not the reason passive subscribers keep paying.

steps.title: Name the inaction
steps.content: Inertia is the tendency to remain unchanged or inactive, which fits subscribers who continue paying after they have stopped valuing the subscription. The answer is A.
